stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 20 observations with mean 51.2 % (within-factory SD = 5.0). factory B has 8 observations with mean 81.1 % (within-factory SD = 5.6). factory C has 20 observations with mean 33.6 % (within-factory SD = 5.3). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.1 and 17.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 51.2 % (shrunk from raw 51.2). Range [51.1, 51.2] as between-factory SD varies from 7.1 to 17.9. Pooled mean: 48.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 48.9 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=11.9: B = 0.991.
Interval [51.1, 51.2] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (51.2) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (48.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
